verdict_reasoning: B2M (beta-2-microglobulin) is a well-characterized non-covalent subunit of MHC class I heterotrimeric complexes displayed on the surface of virtually all nucleated cells. It lacks its own transmembrane anchor but is assembled in the ER with MHC-I heavy chain and co-trafficked to the plasma membrane as a stable complex partner, placing its body on the extracellular face. Multiple PMIDs (37627243, 39057057, 34971932) confirm this canonical cell-surface localization. B2M KO abolishes cell-surface HLA-I expression (PMID 34971932), confirming B2M is integral to the surface complex. Although B2M can dissociate and circulate as a serum protein, the membrane-associated pool is stable and wash-resistant as part of the pMHC-I complex. Importantly, the protein body itself is extracellularly accessible on the surface — it is the subunit that can be targeted by antibodies on intact cells, not merely via pMHC peptide presentation. This fits the stable_complex_partner reason.
user_message:
Triage the human gene **B2M**.

Canonical identifiers and gene summary (machine-resolved from HGNC and NCBI; no further lookups available — judge from the context below plus your trained knowledge):

- HGNC approved name: beta-2-microglobulin
- HGNC symbol: B2M
- UniProt accession: P61769
- Aliases: AMYLD6, IMD43, MHC1D4
- Previous symbols: (none)
- HGNC gene-group memberships: C1-set domain containing
- CD nomenclature: (none assigned)
- NCBI summary: This gene encodes a serum protein found in association with the major histocompatibility complex (MHC) class I heavy chain on the surface of nearly all nucleated cells. The protein has a predominantly beta-pleated sheet structure that can form amyloid fibrils in some pathological conditions. The encoded antimicrobial protein displays antibacterial activity in amniotic fluid. A mutation in this gene has been shown to result in hypercatabolic hypoproteinemia.[provided by RefSeq, Aug 2014]

Emit one JSON object matching the `TriageRecordDraft` schema as your **entire** response — no prose around it, no markdown code fences, no commentary. Required keys: `verdict`, `verdict_reasoning`, `reason`.

Surface-context literature evidence (NCBI PubMed esearch ranked by relevance for gene + surface terms; abstracts efetched and sentence-filtered to those mentioning both the gene and a surface / extracellular / membrane / biotinylation / flow-cytometry keyword):

- [PMID 37627243] Cell Surface B2m-Free Human Leukocyte Antigen (HLA) Monomers and Dimers: Are They Neo-HLA Class and Proto-HLA?
  Cell surface HLA-I molecules (Face-1) consist of a polypeptide heavy chain (HC) with two groove domains (G domain) and one constant domain (C-domain) as well as a light chain, B2-microglobulin (B2m).
- [PMID 37627243] Cell Surface B2m-Free Human Leukocyte Antigen (HLA) Monomers and Dimers: Are They Neo-HLA Class and Proto-HLA?
  However, HCs can also independently emerge unfolded on the cell surface without peptides as B2m-free HC monomers (Face-2), B2m-free HC homodimers (Face 3), and B2m-free HC heterodimers (Face-4).
- [PMID 39057057] Conformational Alterations of the Cell Surface of Monomeric and Dimeric β2m-Free HLA-I (Proto-HLA) May Enable Novel Immune Functions in Health and Disease.
  The HCs can also independently emerge on the cell surface as B2m-free HC monomers without peptides (Face-2).
- [PMID 39057057] Conformational Alterations of the Cell Surface of Monomeric and Dimeric β2m-Free HLA-I (Proto-HLA) May Enable Novel Immune Functions in Health and Disease.
  Early investigators suggested that the occurrence of B2m-free HCs on the cell surface resulted from the dissociation of B2m from Face-1.
- [PMID 39057057] Conformational Alterations of the Cell Surface of Monomeric and Dimeric β2m-Free HLA-I (Proto-HLA) May Enable Novel Immune Functions in Health and Disease.
  However, others documented the independent emergence of B2m-free HCs (Face-2) from the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) to the cell surface.
- [PMID 39057057] Conformational Alterations of the Cell Surface of Monomeric and Dimeric β2m-Free HLA-I (Proto-HLA) May Enable Novel Immune Functions in Health and Disease.
  The clustering of such HC molecules on either the cell surface or on exosomes resulted in the dimerization of B2m-free HCs to form homodimers (if the same allele, designated as Face-3) or heterodimers (if different alleles, designated as Face-4).
- [PMID 34971932] Generation of a B2M homozygous knockout human somatic cell nuclear transfer-derived embryonic stem cell line using the CRISPR/Cas9 system.
  B2M binding to HLA-I proteins is essential for functional HLA-I on the cell surface.
- [PMID 34971932] Generation of a B2M homozygous knockout human somatic cell nuclear transfer-derived embryonic stem cell line using the CRISPR/Cas9 system.
  B2M KO cell line, which does not express HLA-I molecules on cell surface, has pluripotency and differentiation ability to three germ layers.
